title: Data, Database Systems, and the Query Journey · Query Trace: Dbms Purpose
summary: A support organization has inconsistent spreadsheets, duplicated customer facts, and no defensible retention process. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql dbms purpose increment in a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare a DBMS with files and spreadsheets using integrity, concurrency, independence, recovery, and query needs.
Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Dbms Purpose · Theory Dbms Purpose
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory support system has 3 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Dbms Purpose contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Dbms Purpose · Theory Dbms Purpose.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare a dbms with files and spreadsheets using integrity, concurrency, independence, recovery, and query needs. For Predict Dbms Purpose · Theory Dbms Purpose, keep Dbms Purpose connected to executable relational evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits. Apply that move specifically to Dbms Purpose while producing a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record.

steps.title: Build Dbms Purpose evidence · Theory Dbms Purpose
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-dbms-purpose-2i0tm9" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; database-system and data-lifecycle evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Dbms Purpose evidence · Theory Dbms Purpose.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Dbms Purpose into observable performance evidence. For Build Dbms Purpose evidence · Theory Dbms Purpose, keep Dbms Purpose connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-dbms-purpose-2i0tm9
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT name, type
FROM sqlite_schema
WHERE type IN ('table', 'view')
ORDER BY type, name;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
